r: lagānā, lagnā, se, meṅ, lagānā-bujhānā, lagā-denā, lagā-rakhnā
def: v.t. To detain, keep; — to put by, lay by; to hoard up
subs: v.t. (intens.) To put to (a boat); to put in, plant, set, &c. ( = lagānā); — to attach or unite (one, to oneself), to conciliate, to win over; to fold (to one's bosom), to clasp, to embrace
v.t. To impute; to charge (with); to injure by imputation; to calumniate.
ex: اُسے کوئی کام پہ لگا ود
use koī kām pih lagā do
wety: Urdu verb set لگنا لاگنا لگانا لَگوانا (lagvānā) Inherited from Sanskrit लागयति (lāgayati).

def: s.f. dim. of Pers. lagan, q.v.

ety: lagā˚, rt. of lagānā+āw or āʼo = Prk. एअव्यं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: s.m. Attachment, connexion; bond, link; series; adherence, adhesion; contact; intercourse; access; inclination, propensity; relation; reference, allusion; resemblance, analogy; symmetry; — application; bearing, potential bearing (against)
subs: adj. Without connection or tie, free, unencumbered; disconnected, separate; disinterested, impartial.

def: s.m. A seedling (rice or tobacco) transplanted
subs: or lāgaṛ-kā guṛ, s.m. Fine molasses.

ety: prob. akin to lākaṛ or lakkaṛ, q.v.
def: adj. Lean, thin, gaunt, meagre (syn. dublā, patlā).
s.f. Leanness, thinness, gauntness.
v.t. (dialec.) = ragaṛnā, q.v.
s.f. Expense; expenditure, outlay; cost, price, cost-price, prime cost
subs: or lāgat baiṭhnā, or lāgat lagnā, v.n. To cost.
wety: From لَگْنا (lagnā, “to be applied”) + ـَت (-at, nominal suffix).

ety: lag˚, rt. of lagnā + Prk. अंतं = S. अत्; or S. लगितं
def: adv. (dialec.) Connectedly; in close succession, continuously; without intervention (of time or space), uninterruptedly, at the very time (of), instantly.
adj. & adv. See s.v. lagā.
ex: وہ تین دن لگاتار جگا رہا۔
woh tīn din lagātār jagā rahā.
He stayed awake for three days continuously.
wety: لگنا (lagnā) + ـتار (-tār).

ety: lagā˚, rt. of lagānā+ū = Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)त्ट+कः
def: s.m. & f. One who fixes, or applies, &c.; applier, &c. (see lagānā; — syn. lagāne-wālā).
s.m. A lover, paramour ( = lagwā).

ety: Prk. लग्गओ = S. लङ्गकः, (with insertion of व)
def: s.m. A lover, a paramour.

ety: lagāw˚, rt. of lagāwnā+Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: s.f. Something taken with food as a relish; a relish (syn. lāwan).

ety: lagā˚ + āwa = Prk. एअव्वअ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+Prk. ट्ट = S. त्वं
def: s.f. Attachment; adherence; application, &c. ( = lagāʼo, q.v.); intimacy, intimate connexion; a liaison; sexual intercourse.
v.t. = lagānā, q.v.

ety: lag˚, rt. of lagnā + Prk. आइत्त+इओ = S. आपयन् + इकः
def: s.m. Intimate acquaintance, friend.

s.m. Attachment, affection, union, intimacy; secret connexion, a liaison; harmonious relation, &c. ( = lāg, q.v.); compatibility, consistency; similarity, resemblance; competition, rivalry (see lāg); — catch, hold; basis, plan; fixture; preliminary arrangement, first step (towards); commencement, beginning; — a long pole, a prop; a long bamboo (or pole) with which a boat is impelled
subs: (-se), To be compatible (with), be consistent (with); to be comparable (with); to vie or compete (with), to be or become the rival or equal (of)
(-se), To form an attachment or intimacy (with); — to compare (with); — to make the preliminary arrangements (for, -kā); to commence, begin
(-se), To be or become united (to); attachment or intimacy to be formed (with); — to be compared (with); to bear resemblance (to); — to be commenced.

ety: dim. of laggā, q.v.; — ī = Prk. इअ = S. इका
def: s.f. A pole; staff; gibstaff; rod; a fishing-rod; a crook (syn. lagsī); a boat-hook.

s.m. Quickness, swiftness, rapidity, speed; lightness; activity, dexterity, facility, readiness, ease; littleness, smallness, minuteness, delicacy; health; insignificance, meanness; levity, frivolity, inconsiderateness; (in Pros.) shortness (of a vowel or syllable); diminution; contempt; undervaluing.

adj. (f. laghvī), Quick, swift, fleet, rapid; nimble, active; light; easy; (in Prosody) short or light (as a vowel or a syllable); little, small, diminutive; young; weak, feeble; slight, trivial, unimportant, insigniticant; mean, low; vain, frivolous; (in Arith.) a smaller quantity; the lesser root (in an equation)
subs: (laghu+āhār), adj. (f. -ī), Eating little, making a light repast, moderate in diet, temperate, abstemious
(˚ghu+ur˚), s.f. A light or small wave
or laghū-bele, adv. In a short time, shortly
or laghu-patanak, adj. & s.m. Quickly-flying, light-winged; lightly-falling; — name of a crow (in the Hitopadeśa)
adj. (in Alg.) Composed of fewer terms (as a side in an equation)
or laghū-karaṇ, s.m. Reducing in weight or consequence, holding light or cheap, thinking little of, contemning
s.m. A species of fish (prob. a variety of the Pimelodus)
s.m. (lit. 'having light, or easily digested, flesh'), A kind of partridge, the francoline
s.m. (f. -ā), A slight or weak friend, an ally of little power or value
s.m. (in Alg.) The lesser root (of an equation); the least root (with reference to the additive quantities).

def: s.f. = S لگهواهار लघ्वाहार laghvāhār, s.m. See s.v. laghu.
s.f. = S لگهتو लघुत्व laghutva, s.m. Lightness, levity; shortness; smallness, littleness; lowness, meanness; insigniticance, unimportance; diminution; humiliation; wantonness; disregard, disrespect.
s.m. = S لگهتا लघुता laghutā, s.f. Lightness, levity; shortness; smallness, littleness; lowness, meanness; insigniticance, unimportance; diminution; humiliation; wantonness; disregard, disrespect.
s.m. = S لگهورمي लघूर्मि laghūrmi, s.f. See s.v. laghu.
s.f. (m. laghu), A frivolous woman; an insignificant woman; a delicate or beautiful woman; — a light carriage; — urine.
s.f. A mark, a butt; — attachment, affection, love.

ety: fr. lag or lāg, q.v.; — ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. Affection, love; desire, longing; — hunger
subs: To satisfy desire, or a longing or craving.

def: prep. = lag, q.v.; — 2˚ past. conj. part. = lag or lag-ke, qq.v.; — 3˚ adv. = liye, q.v.
postpn. (dialec.) = liye, q.v.
part. (f. -ā), Connected (with), attached (to), &c.
s.m. A stork.
s.f. A (short?) vowel-mark (in the Nāgarī alphabet).

ety: prob. lag = lāgū or laguʼā+S. मात्रकः
def: s.m. Lover, paramour.
s.f. A (short?) vowel-mark (in the Nāgarī alphabet).
v.n. (dialec. or rustic) = lagnā, q.v.

ety: lag˚ = Prk. लग्ग(इ) = S. लग्य(ते), rt. लग्+nā for anā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To be attached (to, -se, or -meṅ), to be joined (to), be connected (with), to be fastened (to, -meṅ); to be moored (a ship); to come to anchor; to be or become fixed (in or on); to come to a stand-still; to be inserted (in); to be planted or set (trees, &c.); to be added (to), to be appended; to fix itself (in or into); to take root, to become rooted (in); to be settled (on or upon); to settle down (in), to make a haunt (of), to hang about, to haunt, to frequent (e.g. wahāṅ sher lagtā hai); to lie in wait; — to be applied (to); to be put, or laid, or imposed (upon), to be staked (on, -par); to be invested (as money); to be spent, or expended, or consumed (on); to cost; to be valued at; — to be put (in or into); to be posted (as a letter); to be disposed of (to, -meṅ), to be sold (to), to be taken (at, — e.g. sau jildeṅ sarkār-meṅ lag-gaʼīṅ); — to be put in order, to be arranged; — to be employed, be occupied or engaged (in); to be intent (on), to be set or bent (on); — to be close (to), be contiguous (to), to adjoin; to join; to touch; to come into contact, or connection, or correspondence (with); to be in close intimacy (with, -se); to enter (into) and form the substance (of), to be duly concocted and assimilated; to be joined in sexual congress, to lie (with, -se); to copulate (with); — to attach (to, -ko), to cling (to); to be incurred, or contracted (as blame, disgrace, &c.); to attach oneself (to), to be with, or on the side (of); — to come (to); to fall (to or together), to close, shut (as doors, shutters, eyelids or eyes, &c.); to fall or draw (in, to collapse, to become shrunk or shrivelled (e.g. māre bhūk-ke peṭ lag-gayā); — to attain or reach (to), to arrive (at); — to stick, adhere (to); to catch; to falter (in speech), to stutter, stammer (e.g. zabān lagnā); — to light (on); to hit or strike (on or against, -meṅ); — to be discovered (a clue or trace, &c.); to act or operate (on, -ko), to take effect (on), to affect; to have the intended or natural effect, to answer, succeed, take, come off; to be achieved, be effected; — to get on foot, to get into vogue or fashion; — to touch, affect, move, melt (with dat.); to come home (to), to tell (upon); — to hurt or wound; to bite or sting; to burn or smart (as anything acrid or pungent); to taste (e.g. khaṭṭā lagnā); to be felt or perceived, to have a sense or feeling (of); to feel, perceive (e.g. sardī lagtī hai); — to act or operate hurtfully or disagreeably (as food, &c.), to be unwholesome (as the water of a place, e.g. pānī wahāṅ-kā lagtā hai); to bear or press (upon), to gall, pinch, rub (as a saddle on the back of a horse, &c.); to receive or sustain a galling or abrasion, to be galled, &c.; — to fall (upon, -ko), to attack (as blight, &c.); to be affected with blemish or damage; to be burnt or scorched (as food in cooking, or clothes in drying before a fire); to be or become touched, or tainted, or corrupted, or rotten, to rot; — to be imputed (to); to be falsely accused; — to be wanting or necessary (to); — to come or arise (upon or to), to happen (to), befall, betide; — to form an affection (of, — e.g. bhūk lagnā, pyās lagnā, &c.), to feel or have the desire (for, — e.g. jhāṛā lagnā, peshāb lagnā); — to be met (with); to be caught or overtaken (by, — as disease, calamity, &c.); to fall in the way (of), come (before), present itself (to); — to be found or obtained (by, — as service or employment); — to arise or come (upon); to be borne or produced (as fruit); to bear (as fruit-trees); to shoot, sprout, germinate; — to be or become, to have some certain condition, or quality, or accident; to be found to be; to look (like), to seem to be; to seem, appear; — to be kindled, to catch, to break out (as fire); to apply (to, -ko), to be applicable or pertinent, to hold good; to apply oneself (to); to be about, to begin (in this sense employed to form Inchoative compounds, e.g. kahne lagā, 'he began to say'); — to arise and proceed; to set in (as rains, cold, heat, &c.); to be proceeding or in progress, to be in full fling or activity (as a market, &c.); — to have suitableness, or agreeableness, or harmonious relation; to fit, suit, become; to refer (to); to bear (on); to be related (to), to be a relation (of, -kā); to appertain (to), to belong (to); to be the concern, or province, or proper office or business (of); to be incumbent or obligatory (on, -ko; to be necessary to be done or suffered (by); — (as a gerund) s.m. Applying, application, &c.
subs: v.n. For this and other similar compounds, see s.v. the past conj. part. lag
v.n. intens. of, and = lagnā (lag-jānā is the more com. form)
(-ke pīche), To follow (in the track of), to go (after).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀮𑀕𑁆𑀕𑀤𑀺 (laggadi), from Sanskrit लग्यति (lagyati). Cognate with Nepali लाग्नु (lāgnu), Punjabi ਲੱਗਣਾ (laggaṇā), Gujarati લાગવું (lāgvũ), Marathi लागणे (lāgṇe), Assamese লগা (loga).

def: s.f. (of lagna, q.v.), A female bard, a poetess.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀮𑀕𑁆𑀕𑀤𑀺 (laggadi), from Sanskrit लग्यति (lagyati). Cognate with Nepali लाग्नु (lāgnu), Punjabi ਲੱਗਣਾ (laggaṇā), Gujarati લાગવું (lāgvũ), Marathi लागणे (lāgṇe), Assamese লগা (loga).

def: part. Attached (to), joined (to), connected (with), applying (to); adhering (to); in contact (with); intent (on), set (upon); assiduously engaged (in), occupied (in), concerned (with); — s.m. A bard, minstrel, panegyrist; — (vulg. लगन lagan), point of contact; point where the horizon and ecliptic (or path of the planets) meet; the oblique ascension; the divisions of the equator as rising in succession with each sign in an oblique sphere; the rising of a sign of the zodiac above the horizon; the sun's entrance (also, the moment of the sun's entrance) into a sign of the zodiac; (hence) an auspicious moment; a moment; the appointing the day of marriage, or the letter or message appointing the day (sent by the bride's father to the father of the bridegroom); — marriage, espousal; nuptials, a wedding
subs: (or -kunḍlī) or lagan-kundalī, s.f. A circle for a horoscope; a horoscope.
ex: مُسَلْمان، ہِنْدُوون کے ساتھ دُرْگاپُوجا میں شَرِیک ہوتے تہے اَور شادی بْیاہ وَغَیرَہ کے مَوقَع پَر بَرَہْمَنوں سے شُبھ لَگَن اَور سَعدِ ساعَت دَریافْت کِیا کَرتے تھے.
Islamic Culture in the Indian Subcontinent
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit लग्न (lagna).

def: s.m. A hyena ( = lakṛā, q.v.).
v.t. (dialec.) = ragṛānā, q.v.
s.f. A long crook-stick (for gathering fruit).

ety: imperf. part. of lagnā, and = lag˚ + Prk. अंतओ = S. अत् (अन्त)+कः
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Touching, joining, &c.; applying (to, -se); having affinity, related; resembling; — pricking; biting; burning; acting or operating injuriously; being unwholesome (as the water of a place, &c.); — s.m. (f. -ī), Assessment, stipulated rent (of land)
subs: adv. Connectedly; in close succession, continuously, uninterruptedly; repeatedly.

def: s.f. Attachment, affection, love, &c. (i.q. lāg, lagan, and lagī, qq.v.).
s.f. (dialec.) = lāg, q.v.
s.m.f. Attached (to); adhering (to), clinging or sticking (to), following; desirous (of); — an adherent, a friend; a supporter, protector; a partizan; a follower, a wooer; a pursuer, persecutor
subs: (kisī-kā or -kī), To cling or stick (to one); to follow or pursue persistently; to persecute; to sue; to woo; to be intent (on); to be attached (to), be a lover (of).

ety: rt. of lagnā+Prk. उअऔ = S. अ +क+कः
def: adj. Close together, contiguous; thick-set, continuous; — beautiful delightful, pleasing, alluring, attractive.
prep. = lag, q.v.

ety: rt. of lagnā+Prk. उअऔ = S. अ +क+कः
def: adj. Close together, contiguous; thick-set, continuous; — beautiful delightful, pleasing, alluring, attractive.

ety: rt. of lagnā+Prk. उअऔ = S. अ +क+कः
def: adj. Close together, contiguous; thick-set, continuous; — beautiful delightful, pleasing, alluring, attractive.
s.m. (East. Hindī) = laguṛ, q.v.
s.m. A stick, staff, club; an iron club, or a club bound with iron; a bar (of iron, or other metal); — a species of hawk or falcon (prob. so called from the bar-like marks or stripes (on its breast)
subs: or lagaṛ-bagghā, or lagaṛ-bhaggā, s.m. A hyena ( = lakaṛ-bagghā, lakṛā, or lagṛā).

def: s.f. (dialec.), Attachment, love.

ety: inf. n. of لغو 'to talk triflingly,' &c.
def: s.m. Frivolous or nonsensical talk, nonsense; thoughtless and improper conversation; gibberish; — adj. Frivolous, trifling, nonsensical, absurd, preposterous, false, improper, contemptible
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لغو (laġw), from Arabic لَغْو (laḡw). Related to لغوی (luğavī, “belonging to a language”).

ety: doub. caus. of lagnā: — rt. lag˚+wa˚ = awā = āwā = āwāw = Prk.अवे or आव redupl. = S. आपि caus. increment, redupl.
def: v.t. To cause to be applied, or put, &c. (to, -meṅ); — to cause to apply, &c.; to apply, put (in), tix, &c. ( = lagānā, q.v.)
s.m. (rustic) = lagwā.
adj. corr. of لغويه lag̠wiya, q.v.

ety: for A. لغوية lag̠wīyat, fem. of lag̠wī, rel. n. fr. lag̠w
def: adj. & s.m. = lag̠w, q.v.
s.m. pl. (of lag̠wīya, q.v.), Frivolities, vain or foolish words or speeches, nonsensical utterances, absurdities, improprieties.
part. (Old H., or Braj) = lagā, q.v.
part. adj. Slipping; tripping, stumbling; — s.m. A slip, stumble, fall.
part. adj. Slipping; slipping down; — slippery.
s.f. Slipping, sliding; stumbling, falling; shaking, tottering, trembling; a slip, slide, &c.; offence; — prevarication, equivocation
subs: To slip, slide, &c. (syn. phisalnā); to make a slip; — to prevaricate.

def: s.m. A kind of fine silk cloth resembling gauze ( = lāhī; Pers. also lāh).
s.m. Lac ( = lākh, q.v.).

ety: Prk. लाहु and लाहओ; S. लाभः and लाभकः
def: s.m. Gain, profit, advantage.
To her.

ety: Prk. लाहु and लाहओ; S. लाभः and लाभकः
def: s.m. Gain, profit, advantage.
s.m. Flame (esp. without smoke); — redness; — flying or ascending (as dust).
s.f. Brilliance, blaze, glare, glitter, flash.

v.n. To glitter, flash, glance, shine, dance (as light); to be kindled or lighted, to rise up into a flame, to blaze up; to rise (as wind); — to bend or wave (as corn or grass before the wind); — to warble, quaver
subs: (or lahak) bolnā, To speak aloud or with confidence.

def: s.m. Yeast or leaven obtained from the bark of the beṛ, pīpal, &c.
s.m. A herd; flock; pack (cf. lehaṅṛā, and leṅḍhā).
s.m. A skirt (it is tied round the loins and extends to the feet or ground).
s.m. A stone on which washermen beat clothes; — a whetstone; — a musical instrument(?).
s.m. Misfortune, calamity.
s.m. = lahaṅḍā, q.v.

s.f. A wave, billow; surge; undulation; a waving line (in patterns of cloth, &c.); a flowing or undulating fold (in draperies, &c.); — whim, fancy, vision, wild fancy, freak; — emotion, excitement, fit of passion; ecstasy, transport, rapture, frenzy; furor; a convulsive or spasmodic affection of the body (as from intoxicating or poisonous substances, from venomous bites or stings, from anger, lust, &c., or as in death); a sudden seizure, a paroxysm, fit; throe, heave; smart, ache, burn; — puff of a zephyr or soft wind; — a term for the lines which lie under the throat of certain snakes (according to the number of these lines, it is supposed, is the number of convulsive throes with which a bitten person is affected)
subs: A wave to rise; emotion, &c. to rise
A wave to come; — to prosper, to be happy
(bahar, fr. bahnā, or a jingling wood), s.f. Prosperity; prime and vigour, fulness and flush, glow and glory, zenith and heyday
(-ko), A paroxysm to come on, to have a sudden seizure, or a fit, &c.
adj. Wavy, undulating, (in Bot.) repand; — fanciful, whimsical, fitful, crotchety; — flashing, fine, pretty
(in Acous.) Rarefaction of a portion of a wave
(in (Acous.) Condensation of a portion of a wave

def: s.m. (dialec.) = laharrā, q.v.

ety: lahar, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To wave, to undulate; to fluctuate; to rise in waves (water); — to blaze up; — to play loosely, to put off, to shuffle; — v.t. To raise delusive hopes in (a person), to excite, to tantalize.
wety: From لَہَر (lahar, “wave”) + ـَانا (-ānā, “verb form”).

def: s.f. A wave; — a convulsive or spasmodic affection (of the body).
v.n. To wave ( = lahrānā), to undulate; to rise in waves (water).
s.f. (dialec.) = naharnī, q.v.

ety: prob. lahar, q.v.+rā = Prk. डओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. A kind of millet, Panicum spicatum (syn. bājrā).

ety: lah˚, prob. = S. लभ; cf. S. लभस
def: s.f. A hawser, a cable; — (dialec.) a large basket.

ety: lah˚, prob. = S. लभ; cf. S. लभस
def: s.f. A hawser, a cable; — (dialec.) a large basket.
prop. n. The city of Lahor, the capital of the Panjāb.
adj. contrac. of lāhaurī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to Lahor; born or produced in Lahor; — a native (or a resident) of Lahor
subs: s.m. Rock or fossil salt from Lahor.

def: s.m. A long loose garment; — a kind of paroquet.
s.m. = laberā, q v.
s.f. A place dug in the side of a grave (in which dead bodies are deposited), a sepulchral niche; (in India) a niche or a hollow in which a corpse is washed; (local) a tomb, grave
subs: (-kī), Filling up with earth the hollow in which a corpse has been washed; filling up the grave of a deceased person (a ceremony performed on the twenty-ninth day after interment).

def: s.f. (dialec.) Wine (cf. lāhan); — sediment, lees.
s.m. = lakherā, q.v.
s.f. A kind of plant; — a kind of fine silk cloth ( = lāh; Pers. lāh, and lāʼe).

ety: act. part. of لحق 'to touch, or reach,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Who or what touches or reaches unto; reaching (to, -ke), overtaking; adhering; adjoining; conjoined, affixed; touching; contiguous; — an ap pendage; an adjunct, accompaniment; a supplement; a dependant; (in Gram.) an affix, a suffix
subs: (-ke), To adhere or stick (to); to reach (to), to overtake; to attach (to); to adjoin; to touch; to accompany.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لاحق, from Arabic لَاحِق (lāḥiq).

def: adj. Flesh-eating, carnivorous.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لَحْم (lahm), from Arabic لَحْم (laḥm).

def: s.m. The tongue; — voice, sound, speech, talk; tone, accent, accentuation; modulation; cadence.

ety: causal. of lahaknā; — lahkā˚ = lahak+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To cause to shine or glitter; to cause to blaze or flame, to make a flame or blaze, to blow up (a fire); — to cause to warble or quaver; to warble, quaver, trill; — to tally-ho.

ety: lah-kār˚ = S. लस+कार; or lahkārnā = next, q.v.
def: To fondle; to pat and encourage (a horse, &c.).
s.m. Rice-milk eaten by (or presented to) a bride and bridegroom.
s.f. Glittering, flashing; glitter, flash; blaze.
adj. (f. -ī), Shining, bright, glittering, resplendent.
adj. (f. -ī), Waving, undulating, bending (as corn, &c. before the wind); — flourishing, blooming, luxuriant.
s.f. Waving or bending (of corn, &c. before the wind).

ety: S. लस, redupl.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment) + nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To wave or bend (as corn, &c. before the wind); — to bloom, to be verdant, to flourish (cf. lahnā).
adj. (f. -ī), Waving, undulating, bending (as corn, &c. before the wind); — flourishing, blooming, luxuriant.
s.f. Stiff or thick mud.
adj. & s.m. Borrowing and never paying; — one who borrows and never pays.
s.m. Flesh, meat (syn. gosht, māṅs); — solder.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لَحْم (lahm), from Arabic لَحْم (laḥm).

def: s.m. corr. of لمحه lamḥa, q.v.
s.f. corr. of لکشمي lakshmī, q.v.
s.f. Sound, voice; note, tone; modulation, melody.

ety: lah˚ = Prk. लह(इ) = लभ(ते), rt. लभ्
def: v.t. To find, get, experience; — v.n. To get on well, to prosper, flourish; — to accrue; to avail, answer, boot, signify; — s.m. Profit, gain; outstanding debt; — lot, portion, fortune, fate, destiny
s.m. A quaver, shake, trill (in music); — a trilling lay, a lively air or tune; — (dialec.) a skirt (syn. lahangā)
subs: or lahrā bajānā, To play a lively tune; — to give (one) a shoe-beating
To raise visions or hopes (of, -kā, a thing, in, -meṅ, a person), to excite, to tantalize; — to play fast and loose (with); to put off, evade; to avoid performing an agreement.

def: adj. & s.m. Fanciful, whimsical, capricious, fitful, inconstant, fickle; emotional; — merry, gay, jovial; — odd, fantastic; — careless, inconsiderate, thoughtless; — a fanciful (or an emotional) person, &c.; a merry-andrew, a clown.
adj. Wavy; waved (as silk, &c.), watered (silk, &c.); serpentine; striped; — s.m. Waved or watered silk; wave-like embroidery on cloth.
s.m. = rahṛū, q.v.
s.m. Garlic, Allium sativum; — a freckle; a blotch; a wrinkle.
wety: From Sanskrit लशुन (laśuna).

def: adj. Garlicky

ety: lahsan+iyā = Prk. इओ = S. इकः; but cf. the preceding
def: adj. Garlicky; — s.m. A certain precious stone, 'cat's eye.'
s.m, = lasoṛā, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = lāh = lābh, qq.v.
To him or it.
s.m. = lohū, q.v.
wety: First attested in c. 1500 as Middle Hindi لہو (luhū /⁠lhū⁠/) / لوہو (lohū), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀮𑁄𑀳𑀺𑀅 (lohia), ultimately from Sanskrit लोहित (lṓhita), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hráwdʰitas, from Proto-Indo-European *h₁rewdʰ- (“red”).

def: s.m. A species of plant.
adj. & s.m. = lohrā, or lauhrā, q.v.

ety: lāh, 'God'+ūt, aff. borrowed from the Aramaic
def: s.f. The divine nature, divinity.
s.m. Playing amusing oneself, passing the time agreeably, unbending the mind; play; anything ludicrous or jocular; whatever diverts the thoughts from anything serious
subs: s.m. Play, sport, game, pastime, mirth, amusement, fun; frivolity
wety: First attested in c. 1500 as Middle Hindi لہو (luhū /⁠lhū⁠/) / لوہو (lohū), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀮𑁄𑀳𑀺𑀅 (lohia), ultimately from Sanskrit लोहित (lṓhita), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hráwdʰitas, from Proto-Indo-European *h₁rewdʰ- (“red”).

ety: inf. n. of لحظ; cf. liḥāz̤
def: s.f. Looking through half-shut eyes; looking askew or through the outer corner of the eye.

ety: for A. لحظة laḥz̤at, n. of un. fr. laḥz̤, q.v.
def: s.m. A look, a glance; — a moment, the twinkling of an eye; a minute
subs: adv. From moment to moment, every moment

def: s.f. Parched grain; a kind of sweetmeat, parched rice mixed with sugar; — wild rice.
s.f. = lāʼonī, q.v.
part. adj. Shining, flashing; bright, splendid; — appearing; apparent, evident, clear.

ety: for A. لائحة lāʼiḥat, fem. of lāʼiḥ
def: s.m. A shining or flashing thing or object; anything evident; — clearness; — splendour; proof.

ety: part. act. of ليق 'to be fit, proper,' &c.
def: adj. Fit (for, -ke), proper, meet; adapted, suitable, apposite, becoming, decent; worthy, deserving, due; able, capable, competent, qualified; convenient; congruous, consistent
subs: adj. Deserving of reward, or of a present or gift
adj. Worthy of acceptance or reception
adj. Fit to be chosen, eligible
adj. Worthy of approbation or commendation, deserving of approval; to be accepted, or granted, or conceded; admissible
adj. Deserving of punishment, punishable, penal
adv. In a proper manner, properly, suitably
That cannot be accepted or received or entertained, inadmissible
or lāʼiq-wālā, adj. Pleonastic forms for lāʼiq
(-ke), To be worthy or deserving (of), to deserve, merit; to suit, fit, become, &c.

def: (for A. لائقة lāʼiqat, fem. of lāʼiq), adj. f. = lāʼiq, q.v.
s.f. Worthiness, fitness, propriety, capability, &c. (see nā-lāʼiqī).
s.f. Night
subs: s.f. Night and day; — a species of wood.

def: s.f. Name of the celebrated mistress of Majnūn (the loves of Majnūn and Laile are celebrated in a fine Persian poem by Nazāmī); — a mistress, sweetheart.
s.f. A night
subs: 'The night of power' (the twenty-seventh of the month Ramaẕān), in which, according to Moḥammadan belief, the Qorʼān began to descend.

def: s.f. A night
subs: 'The night of power' (the twenty-seventh of the month Ramaẕān), in which, according to Moḥammadan belief, the Qorʼān began to descend.

def: adj. & s.m. Of or relating to night, nocturnal; — one who does anything by night, a night-worker, &c.

ety: act. part. of لأم 'to be ignoble,' &c.
def: s.m. Blamer, censurer, reprover; accuser of anything mean or criminal (see laʼīm).
adj. See لئيم laʼīm.
adj. & s.m. Vile, abject, mean, base, worthless, reprehensible, inglorious, ignoble; — sordid, avaricious, miserly; a vile or worthless fellow; a sordid man, a miser.

ety: S. लायनं or लायम्, rt. ली
def: adj. Bent, crooked (timber, &c.).
s.f. Line, row, file (of soldiers); boundary-line, limit (as of an encampment, or of the customs, &c.); — barracks, cantonment
subs: conj. part. Having formed a line or row; — adv. In a line or row; in file
To form a line or row
s.f. (lit. 'the ropes, &c. for laying out an encampment'), The advance guard, tents, &c. which precede a body of troops to lay out an encampment.

def: adj. Accursed, anathematized, execrable; detested, abominable (syn. maʻlūn).
s.m. = lahnā, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = lahaṅgā, q.v.

adj. Attained to perfection, perfected, completed, finished; ready, prepared (for); dressed; concluded (as a bargain); bought, &c.; — s.m. That by which perfection is attained; an arrow for practising with, an arrow with a knob or button on the head (or with a blunt head, syn. tukkā); — a kind of vinegar (in this sense, perhaps fr. S. amla-rasa); — a sprig, twig (prob. = les, 'a small part').
s.f. Lace.
s.f. Procrastination, prevarication, evasion, subterfuge, chicane
subs: or lait-o-laʻal-meṅ ḍālnā, To prevaricate, to hum and ha; to dilly-dally; to shuffle.

def: s.f. Shame, sense of decency; bashfulness, modesty; — honour, reputation, good name
subs: or lāj lagnā (-ko), or lāj khānā, v.n. To feel ashamed; to take shame to oneself
or lāj khonā, or lāj gaṅwānā, v.n. To throw away all shame, to be shameless
(-kī), To have a sense of shame; — to hide the shame (of); to protect the honour (of)
or lājoṅ marnā, v.n. To die of shame, to feel extremely ashamed
v.n. To show a sense of shame; to be ashamed, to be bashful
(f. -vatī), or lāj-want, adj. (f. -ī), Ashamed, abashed, shamefaced, bashful, modest, coy, blushing; chaste, decent.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi لاج (laj /⁠lāj⁠/) (c. 1564) / لج (lj /⁠lajj⁠/) (c. 1609) / لجہ (ljh /⁠lajja⁠/) (c. 1599) / لجا (lja /⁠lajjā⁠/), from Prakrit 𑀮𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀸 (lajjā), from Sanskrit लज्जा (lajjā).

def: s.m. Fried or parched grain; wetted or sprinkled grain.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi لاج (laj /⁠lāj⁠/) (c. 1564) / لج (lj /⁠lajj⁠/) (c. 1609) / لجہ (ljh /⁠lajja⁠/) (c. 1599) / لجا (lja /⁠lajjā⁠/), from Prakrit 𑀮𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀸 (lajjā), from Sanskrit लज्जा (lajjā).

def: s.f. Fried or parched grain ( = lāwā).
s.f. (dialec.) = lāj, or lajjā, qq.v.

ety: for A. لجاجة, inf. n. of لجّ
def: s.f. Importunity, entreaty; adulation.
s.f. The sensitive plant, Mimosa natans or pudica.

ety: lajā˚ for lajjā, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk.अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To be ashamed (of, -se), to be abashed, to take shame to oneself; to blush; — v.t. To make ashamed, to put to shame.
adj. Ashamed, &c. ( = lajālū); shameful, disgraceful.
adj. & s.f. = lajjā-vatī, q.v.s.v. lajjā.
s.m. Azure; — lapis lazuli.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لاجورد.

def: s.m. Stickiness, glutinousness, viscosity; tenacity; clogginess; clamminess; sweltering state (syn. lajlajā); — (dialec.) semen.

ety: lāj = S. लज्जा+Prk. इल्लओ = S. इल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Ashamed, shamefaced, shy, bashful, modest.
s.f. Shame, bashfulness, modesty ( = lāj, q.v.)
subs: (˚jā+an˚), adj. Possessed of shame, &c.( = lajjā-vān, q.v.)
s.m. (in Gram.) Any word expressive of shame or disgust (as ćhī, dhik, phiś)
adj. (f. -ā), Destitute of shame, devoid of modesty, shameless, immodest, impudent, bold, unblushing, forward
adj. (f. -ā), Of a modest disposition, modest, bashful, ashamed
or lajjā-kār, or lajjā-kārī, adj. Causing or occasioning shame, shameful
or lajjā-mant (f. -matī); and lajjā-vat, or lajjā-vān, or lajjā-vant, vulg. lajā-want (f. -vatī), adj. Possessed of shame, full of shame, ashamed, abashed, shamefaced, shy, bashful, modest, blushing
vulg. lajā-wantī, s.f. A bashful woman or girl; the sensitive plant Mimosa natans, or M. pudica

def: adj. Bashful, modest, shy, shameful; — s.m. The sensitive plant, Mimosa natans, or M. pudica.
adj. = lajjā-mān, q.v.s.v. lajjā.
s.f. corr. of لذت laz̤z̤at, q.v.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Ashamed, abashed, modest, bashful, blushing
subs: (-ko), To make ashamed, to put to shame, to shame.

def: s.m. Name of the inventor of chess; an instructor at dice or any game of hazard.
v.n. To become soft or flabby; — v.t. To soften (cf. laćlaćānā).

ety: Prk. लज्जणअं; S. लज्जनीयं, rt. लज्ज्
def: v.n. To feel ashamed or abashed; to be bashful, or shy, &c.; to blush.
v.t. To cause to be ashamed, to put to shame, to shame, to cause to blush.
adj. See s.v. lāj, 'shame.'
adj. & s.f. = lajjā-vatī, q.v.s.v. lajjā.
adj. Made of, or consisting of, lapis lazuli.
s.f. = lajjā, q.v.
v.n. = lajānā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = lakh = lākh, q.v.: — lak-patī = lakh-pati.

ety: for lakkh or lakh = S. लक्ष्यं; or S. लशं, for लसं; — Pers. also lak, 'clean; bare; bald,' see laq-o-daq
def: adj. & s.m. (dialec.) Clean; bright, shining, sparkling, &c.; — sparkle, flash, sudden gleam, coruscation
subs: (Dakh.), adv. With a sudden flash or gleam; instantaneously; in a shake or trice
(-ko), To make clean, to cleanse, purify; to make clear or bright, to polish, burnish
v.n. To shine, glitter, sparkle, glisten, flash, coruscate.

def: s.m. Lac; a kind of lake produced from lac (used for dyeing red); sealing-wax; — a parget-trough; a kneading-trough; — a tortoise
s.m. A mark, spot, blot, stain.

ety: prob. fr. A. liqā, pl. of laqwat or liqwat, 'an eagle'
def: s.m. The Brahminy kite, Haliastur indus; — the fan-tail pigeon.
s.m. A title, an appellation of honour; a surname; a by-name, a nickname.
s.m. A kind of bread-fruit tree, Artocarpus lacucha, and its fruit.

ety: prob. fr. A. lakd, 'striking with hand or foot'
def: s.f. A kick; a cuff; a blow
subs: s.f. Kicking cuffing; — adj. Kicked; trodden upon.

def: = H لاکڙو लाकड़ू lākaṛū, dialec. forms of lakkaṛ and lakṛī, qq.v.
s.m. A hyena (syn. lagṛā, and lagaṛ-bhaggā).

s.f. A stick, staff; wood, timber; fire-wood; — a stalk; — the tough stringy substance in the heart of some fusiform roots (as carrots, turnips, &c.); — adj. Like a stick; stiff, rigid; — emaciated, thin, lean
subs: (-ko), To place wood on the funeral pyre (of)
'To drive everyone with the same stick'; to treat all alike; to fail in showing the consideration due to each one's position
To dry up to a stick, to become lean or emaciated.

def: = H لاکڙ लाकड़ lākaṛ, dialec. forms of lakkaṛ and lakṛī, qq.v.

ety: the Ar. form of P. lakāt
def: adj. Lean; infirm.
s.m. (f.?), Gum-lac, a kind of wax formed by the Coccus lacca; — sealing-wax
subs: adj. (f. -kī), Of lac, made of lac
(-meṅ), To apply sealing-wax (to), to seal; to shut up; to conceal

def: adj. & s.m. A hundred thousand, a lac; an immense number of; any number of
subs: 'With a hundred thousand lives'; with all one's heart, most willingly or cheerfully
'To be possessed of a hundred thousand heads'; to be very persistent, or very pig-headed; to insist; to turn a deaf ear (to)
'To be of the weight of a hundred thousand mans or 'maunds'; to be of immense weight, to be exceedingly heavy; to be (a man) of great weight or consideration
adj. Hundreds of thousands of, lacs of, in (or by) lacs, many lacs
adv. In the presence (or face) of lacs of (people), before any number of people, before everybody, in public; — certainly, assuredly.

ety: used as first member of compounds
def: s.m. Owner of a lac (of rupees), a millionaire; — adj. Rich, wealthy
subs: or lakh-lūṭ, adj. & s.m. Extravagant, wasteful; excessive, over-abundant, copious; — a squanderer, a spendthrift, a prodigal.

def: s.m. Lac-dye (with which women paint their lips)
subs: or lākhā lagānā (-meṅ), To apply lākhā (to the lips).

def: s.f. Seeing; appearing, appearance (syn. dikhāʼī).
s.m. (dialec.) 1˚ = lakshaṇ, q.v.; — 2˚ = lakshmaṇ, q.v.; — 3˚ = lakhnā, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.) 1˚ = lakshmaṇā; — 2˚ = lakhnā, qq.v.
v.t. To cause to see or perceive, to show, to point out, &c. (syn. dikhānā).
s.m. = lakhāwaṭ.
s.f. The act of seeing or perceiving.
s.m. (dialec.) Mark, sign, &c. (syn. ćinh).

ety: lakhā˚, rt. of lakhānā+ū = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: adj. To be seen or perceived; visible, perceptible, discernible; significant.
adj. Besmeared (or coated) with lac
subs: (-ko), To smear with lac; to glaze.

def: v.t. = lakhānā, q.v.
s.f. The act of seeing or perceiving.
s.m. One who smears (or coats) with lac; a varnisher; one who sells things made of lac.
s.f. The wife of a lakherā; a woman who sells things made of lac.

ety: Prk. लक्खिओ; S. लाक्षिकः; and Prk. लक्खिआ; S. लाक्षिका
def: adj. Of lac, made of lac; coloured or dyed with lac; — s.f. A red dye extracted from lac.
s.f. (dialec.) = lakshmī, q.v.
s.f. (Dakh. or dialec.) = lakīr, q.v.

ety: lakh˚, rt. of lakhnā, q.v. + S. इकः
def: s.m. A seer, beholder, spectator, on-looker; — a gad-about.
adj. Weak, feeble, languid, lean.
s.m. = لخلخه laḵẖlaḵẖa, q.v.
s.m. An aromatic unguent; a species of strong perfume; — a censer; a lamp.

ety: prob. fr. S. लष् by redupl.
def: v.n. To gasp or pant with heat or thirst (as birds, &c.).
v.n. To pant from thirst or hunger; to be reduced to extreme feebleness through hunger.
s.m. corr. of lakshmaṇ, q.v.

ety: Prk. लक्खणअं = लक्खणिअं = S. लक्षणीयं, rt. लक्ष्
def: v.t. To see, look at, behold, perceive; to understand; — v. int. To appear, seem.
v.t. To apply lac to.
s.m. Lakhnau (vulg. Lucknow), the capital of Awadh (or Oude).
s.m. See s.v. lakh.
s.m. See s.v. lakh.
s.m. A piece, bit, part, portion; somewhat, some, a little
subs: or laḵẖt-ě-dil, 'A piece of (one's) liver or heart,' a darling child; my darling
adv. In the piece; in a body, en masse, all at once.

ety: lākh, q.v. + wāṅ = Prk. अमओ, or अवँओ = S. तम+कः
def: adj. (f. -wīṅ), Hundred-thousandth.
conj. But, &c. (see ليکن lekin, which is the com. form).

ety: S. लेखा + īr = Prk. इल्ली or इली = S. इल+ई; cf. līk
def: s.f. A line, lineament; a streak, stripe
subs: or lakīr-par faqīr honā, To follow in the track of ancestors; to walk in the beaten path; to move in a groove; to be a slave to old customs or rites; — to make fruitless search for a thing which is lost (com. used in connection with the word sāṅp, 'snake'; e.g. sāṅp nikal-gayā hai, ab lakīr pīṭte haiṅ)
(-par), To draw a line; to mark a boundary; to mark out, strike out (with a pen, &c.); — to renounce, to abjure
s.f. Touching line, tangent (of a circle).

def: v.t. To mark with a line or lines; to mark off; to delineate, to sketch, to make a plan of.
s.m. A piece, lump.
s.m. corr. of laqā or laqqā, q.v.
s.m. A cudgel; a beam; log; block; a bar; timber; wood
subs: s.m. A stockade; a palisade; a barricade; a paling

r: lakkh, lakh, lak, lakh karnā, lakh-lakh karnā
def: (dialec.) = lak, q.v. (used in comp., e.g. lakh karnā, and lakh-lakh karnā).
s.m. corr. of lakshaṇ, q.v.
adj. Possessing a lac (of rupees); worth a lac; very valuable.
s.m. A stork.

ety: for A. لقلقة laqlaqat, inf. n. of لقلق 'to cry tremulously,' &c.
def: s.m. Cackling, or chattering (a stork); the voice of a stork; a continual motion of the jaw or of the tongue (in serpents); — eloquence; pronunciation; — spirit, ambition; — weight, influence (e.g. yěh baṛe laqlaqe-kī 'aurat hai).
v.n. (dialec.), 1˚ = lak-lak karnā, q.v.s.v. lak; — 2˚ = lakhlakhānā, q.v.

ety: in P. also laq-o-taq; fr. laq = lak, 'bald; bare'+A. daqq, 'beaten, bruised, rubbed'
def: adj. Desert, waste, desolate, wild, dreary, bleak and barren.
s.m. contrac. of lakh-paṭi, q.v.s.v. lakh.
s.m. A cudgel; a beam; log; block; a bar; timber; wood
subs: s.m. A stockade; a palisade; a barricade; a paling

def: s.m. A hyena (syn. lagṛā, and lagaṛ-bhaggā).

s.f. A stick, staff; wood, timber; fire-wood; — a stalk; — the tough stringy substance in the heart of some fusiform roots (as carrots, turnips, &c.); — adj. Like a stick; stiff, rigid; — emaciated, thin, lean
subs: (-ko), To place wood on the funeral pyre (of)
'To drive everyone with the same stick'; to treat all alike; to fail in showing the consideration due to each one's position
To dry up to a stick, to become lean or emaciated.

def: s.f. The red animal dye called 'lac'; — the insect which produces lac (it is analogous to the cochineal insect, and, like it, forms, when dried and prepared, a dye of a red colour; its nest is formed of a resinous substance which is used as sealing-wax, and is called shell-lac, vulg. shellac)
subs: s.m. The red Lodh-tree (from the bark of which an astringent infusion is prepared which is used to fix colour in dyeing)
(for lākshā-gr̤ih), s.m. Name of an ancient palace near Benares
s.m. The tree Butea frondosa (syn. palās).

def: s.m. One hundred thousand, a lac; — a mark, butt; object of aim; — disguise, fraud
subs: (˚sha+av˚), adv. Up to a hundred thousand.

def: s.m. A mark, spot; distinctive mark, sign, symptom, token, indication, characteristic; feature; character, attribute, quality; destiny; — accurate description, definition; a predicate; designation, appellation, name; — the Indian crane.
s.f. Indirect indication or hint; metonymy; metaphorical sense (as distinguished from the literal); the female of the Indian crane; a goose.
s.f. (dialec.) = lakshmī, q.v.
part. (f. -ā), Beheld, looked upon; seen, perceived, observed; discerned, discovered; known (from signs, indications, or appearances); — marked.

s.m. Name of a son of Daśaratha by his wife Su-mitrā (he was the younger half-brother and faithful companion of Rāmćandra during his travels and adventures); a proper name; — the Indian crane, Ardea sibirica; — a mark, sign, token, spot ( = lakshaṇ); — a name; — adj. (f. -ā), Possessed of lucky signs or marks, lucky, fortunate, prosperous.
s.f. The female of the Indian crane; — name of a wife of Kr̤ishṇa and mother of Śāmba; a proper name.

s.f. Good fortune, good-luck, fortune, prosperity; wealth, riches; success, happiness; beauty, grace, loveliness, charm, splendour; — royal power, dominion; superhuman power; — a pearl; — name of the goddess of fortune and beauty (she is regarded as the wife of Vishṇu or Nārāyaṇ, and is one of the principal female deities of the Hindūs; she is also called Padmā, Kamalā and Śrī, and is supposed to be the Ceres of the ancients); — an epithet of Sītā, the wife of Rāma
subs: s.m. 'The husband or lord of Lakshmī or Fortune,' a name of Vishṇu; lord or possessor of fortune or wealth, a wealthy man

s.f. 'The worship of Lakshmī,' name of a festival on the fifteenth day in the dark half of the month Kārtik, Sept.-Oct. (said to be celebrated, in modern times, by bankers and traders, to propitiate Fortune); — worship of Lakshmī by the bridegroom and bride after the bride has been brought to her father-in-law's house
s.f. 'Shadow (or protection or favour) of Lakshmī'; good fortune, prosperity, success
(kisī-ke) ghar-meṅ ānā or utarnā, 'Fortune to come to the house' (of); to enjoy good fortune or prosperity, to prosper, thrive; to have a run of good luck
(-ko), To offer (a meal, &c.) to Lakshmī and Nārāyaṇ before commencing (to eat, &c.); to begin to eat (syn. bismillāh karnā)
adj. (f. -vatī), Fortunate, prosperous, thriving; wealthy; handsome, beautiful.

def: adj. & s.m. To be marked; to be seen or noted; observable, perceptible, visible; recognizable; — an aim, object (to be aimed at), butt, target; — mere appearance, sham, pretence, fraud, disguise; — a lac, a hundred thousand.
s.m. Lac (the animal dye); — a rag, &c. ( = lattā, q.v.).
s.f. corr. of lagtī, q.v.
s.m. Stick, staff, cudgel, club, bludgeon.

ety: lakuṭ, q.v. + S. इका; Prk. इआ
def: s.f. A small club or bludgeon (especially the club held by Krishṇa).
s.m. Spasmodic distortion of the face or mouth; — paralysis
subs: (-ko), or laqwa ho ā (-ko), To be stricken with paralysis, to have a paralytic stroke, to be paralyzed.

ety: from the Persian; prob. akin to S. रक्त
def: adj. Red (syn. surḵẖ), ruddy; red-hot; inflamed; enraged; — s.m. A ruby (cf. laʻl); the male of the bird Fringilla amandava (cf. muniyā); — the seed of Abrus precatorius (syn. ghuṅgćī)
subs: or lāl-surḵẖ, or lāl-lāl, adj. Very red; red-hot; fiery, flushed with passion
adj. Flaming red; fiercely or vividly red
v.n. To become (or turn) red, to redden; to become red-hot; to flush; to get into a rage
'To show red and yellow eyes'; to glare fiercely; to fly into a passion
or lāl ćitr-mol, s.m. Plumbago rosea
v.n. To flourish, to be prosperous, to be happy (e.g. lāl raho, 'Continue flourishing! be prosperous!')
s.m. Red amaranth (used as a vegetable), Amaranthus paniculatus, or A. gangeticus
or lāl kaddū, s.m. The red pumpion, Curcurbita maxima
s.f. The red book; a book to which an ignoramus refers for the solution of every difficulty; — a book of reference; — lāl kurtī, s.f. A red coat; — the European infantry lines in an Indian cantonment
A regiment of red-coats, i.e. of British infantry
s.f. Red oleander, or rose-bay, Nerium odorum
s.f. Red pepper; cayenne; red chilli, capsicum
v.n. To become red, &c. ( = lāl paṛnā, q.v.); — to be on the point of winning (at play); to be 'hot,' or on the point of discovering anything (as a hidden handkerchief, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لَال (lāl).

ety: Pers. also lāl; prob. S. लाल, fr. caus. of rt. लल्; cf. lāṛ and lāṛlā
def: adj. Beloved, darling, dear, precious; — dumb; — s.m. An infant boy, a son; a darling, a pet; — a proper name (among Hindūs)
subs: s.m. An ignorant (or a stupid) fellow who pretends to knowledge or acuteness, an ignoramus, a jackanapes, wiseacre
or lāl-gurū, s.m. The priest of the sweeper caste
adj. & s.m. Most dear; very dear; — the dearest of dear ones.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لَال (lāl).

def: s.m. (f.?) = lār, and lālā, 'spittle,' q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لَال (lāl).

def: s.m. A ruby; — any bright gem; (met.) the lips (of a mistress)
subs: v.n. 'To cast up rubies'; (ironic.) to utter (or pour forth) abusive or scurrilous words (e.g. bas, ab ziyāda laʻl na uglo)

def: s.f. Saliva, spittle, slaver, mucous
subs: s.m. (lit. 'having saliva for food'), Name of the hell appropriated to those who eat their meals without first offering food to the gods, to the manes, or to guests
s.m. Passing mucous urine; mucous urine.

ety: from the Pers.; prob. akin to S. लाल, fr. the caus. of rt. लल्
def: s.m. Sir, master; a school-master; a grandee; a respectable Hindū (as a banker, &c.); the designation of a class of Kāyaths who are schoolmasters; a writer, clerk, copyist; a title given to persons of the Vaiśya class, &c.; a respectful term of address to a father, or a father-in-law; (for lallā or lalā, q.v.) dear boy, darling; — (in Persian) the chief (or an upper) servant (intrusted with the education of his master's sons); a major domo; — a slave
subs: a babbler; — babbling, prating, talkativeness, frivolous talk; — a kind of grain resembling sesame; — a kind of herb brought from the neighbourhood of Mecca (the smell of which is said to keep off the piles)
s.m. An affectionate (or a respectful) form of address (commonly applied to Banyās)
(-ko), To sing or pat to sleep, to soothe or coax to sleep
s.m. A form of address (used towards a respectable man not of high rank); Master, Sir; father, father-in-law.

def: adj. Bright, shining, resplendent (pearls).
s.m. = لالا lālā, q.v.
s.m. A tulip; (in India, also) the red poppy; — a glass shade
subs: or lāla-ruḵẖsār, or lāla-ʻiẕār, adj. Tulip-cheeked, red-cheeked
s.m. A bed or garden of tulips

def: s.m. corr. of P. لاله lāla, q.v.

ety: Prk. ललओ, or लल्लओ?; S. लड or लल+कः
def: s.m. A boy; dear boy, darling; — a dolt, blockhead, fool ( = S. laṭa); — adj. (f. -ī), Impotent
subs: A Badāʼuṅ fool; a great dolt or blockhead (cf. the Eng. 'Essex calf').

def: s.m. Longing, eager desire; covetousness, cupidity, greed, avarice; selfishness; occasion; view; want; — incentive to cupidity, a bait, lure
subs: (-ko), To appeal to the cupidity of, to offer a bait (to), to tempt, allure. entice, decoy
(-kā), To be greedy (of or after), to covet; to practise avarice, to be avaricious
(-ke), To be moved by desire or greed (for), to take a bait.

ety: Prk. लल्लक्क; prob. fr. S. लल+कृ
def: s.f. A sudden gush (of any liquid); — eager desire, longing, hankering, craving; — whim, fancy, caprice; — zeal, ardour, enthusiasm.

ety: fr. the caus. of rt. lal
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Caressing, fondling, coaxing, wheedling, seducing, seductive; — a coaxer, wheedler, seducer
subs: s.f. A seductive woman, a wanton.

def: v.n. To be possessed with a longing (for), to long or crave (for); to be greedy (for), to covet; — v.t. To attack, to fall upon.
s.m. A mark or spot on the forehead; — a mark, spot; — an ornament for the forehead; — ornament, decoration, embellishment; — a banner, flag; — a line, row; — a mane; tail.
s.m. 1˚ — lalām, q.v.; — 2˚ corr. of nīlām, q.v.
s.m. A chaplet or wreath of flowers worn on the forehead.
s.m. = lalām, q.v.
s.f. A kind of ear-ornament.
adj. Caressing, fondling, indulging, spoiling; coaxing, wheedling; — s.m. The act of caressing, fondling, petting, &c. &c.; — s.f. A mistress, sweetheart
s.m. Play, sport, pastime; pleasure; dalliance; — the playing or moving to and fro of the tongue; lolling the tongue; — the tree Shorea robusta.
s.f. A woman, a wife; — a playful woman; a woman of pleasure, a wanton woman.
s.m. A boy ( = lalā).
s.m. = lalāṭ, q.v.
s.f. Longing, ardent desire, eager longing for an absent person or object; covetousness (see lālać); the longing of a pregnant woman; regret, sorrow; missing; asking (for), soliciting; dalliance.
adj. Very desirous or wishful.

s.m. Forehead, brow; — (met.) fate, destiny, fortune (conceived, by the Hindūs, to be written on the forehead); — lalāṭ-rekhā, s.f. A line on the forehead (supposed to indicate long life); a wrinkled or corrugated brow; — a coloured sectarial mark (worn by Hindūs) on the forehead.

adj. Relating to the forehead; being on the forehead; relating to fate, arising from destiny; — looking attentively at the forehead (of a master), attentive; — s.m. An attentive servant; — a careless person; an idler.
s.f. An ornament worn on the forehead; a mark made with sandal on the forehead.
s.m. (rustic) A boy, &c. ( = lalā, q.v.).

ety: lālać, q.v. + ā˚ = āw = Prk. आवे or आव = S. आपि;+nā = anā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To be possessed with a longing (for, -par), to long (for), hanker (after), lust (for); to be tantalized; — v.t. To excite desire in; to rouse the cupidity of; to tantalize.
part. adj. Desired, longed for, coveted.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Covetous, cupidinous, greedy, grasping, a varicious, miserly, niggardly, selfish, interested; — a grasping or selfish man, &c.

r: lal-ćitrā, lāl, ćitrā, Plumbago rosea, lal-ćiṭā
def: (i.e. lāl+ćitrā), s.m. Plumbago rosea (also written lal-ćiṭā).
s.m. The plant and flower Nerium odorum.
v.n. To long distractingly and hopelessly (for, -ke); to be despondent, to despair (of); — to be or become unattainable; to be scarce or scanty.

ety: lāl+gānḍ+S. कः, and इका, lit. 'having a red anus'
def: A monkey.

ety: lāl+gānḍ+S. कः, and इका, lit. 'having a red anus'
def: A monkey.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī) = lālak, q.v.

ety: fem. of lalā, q.v.; — ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A girl.
